series_metadata.series_description: Tuition is an amount of money paid to an educating district from a resident district to offset the educating district’s cost of educating a student who is a legal resident of another school district. This includes forms SF-14 (Tuition Adjustment Expenditure), SF-14H (Tuition Adjustment), SF-14Y (Department of Youth Services), SF-DRC (District of Residence Change) Form, MO-8 and SF-14PD (Private Treatment Facilities). Some of these forms are accessible through the Ohio District Data Exchange (ODDEX) system.

retention_rules.disposition: Paper: Retain 5 years, then destroy. ; Electronic: Retain 5 years, then destroy., Paper: Shred ; Electronic: Delete
